title: Research
text: My research lies at the intersection of statistics and biology, with a focus on developing and applying statistical methods for complex, high-dimensional data. I also have active interests in statistics and mathematics education research.

title: Fred Boehm
text: I am an Assistant Professor of Statistics at South Dakota State University. I develop statistical methods for human genetics data, with an emphasis in genome-wide association studies and rare variant association studies. The high dimensionality of genetics data provides many statistical and computing challenges. I try to leverage Bayesian statistical methods, for example, to compute genetic risk predictors for chronic diseases. I enjoy collaborative data science research projects, and I’m always looking for new opportunities.

title: Welcome to My Blog
text:
Welcome to my blog! I plan to write here about statistics, research, teaching, comedy, and anything that captures my attention.
Topics I expect to cover:

Statistical methods in genetics and genomics
Causal inference in observational studies
Teaching statistics and mathematics
R packages and reproducible research workflows
Notes from conferences and seminars

Stay tuned.
